_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Strengthen the 78 highest-priority assertion-scanner baseline entries (71 no-assert + 7 mock-only) and remove them from tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt

steps.description: RED-first: dump the exact 71 `no-assert` entries via `grep '# no-assert$' tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt` and record the list verbatim into the S01 report as `tdd_red_evidence` (these entries are by construction tests that cannot fail). For each of the 71 entries, choose one action: (a) STRENGTHEN (preferred) — add at least one real, specific, behaviour-pinning assertion that would fail if the production code line the test ostensibly covers regressed (mutation-test heuristic in `skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md` §0); (b) DELETE — only if the test is provably checking nothing of value AND the surface is already covered by another existing test, with a one-line rationale in the report naming the covering test verbatim (`DELETE tests/<path>::<test_name> — covered by tests/<other>::<other_test>`); (c) CONVERT — rare, used when the test was mis-categorised as `no-assert` but is actually a mock-only test in disguise; rewrite to assert on a real observable (return value, DB row, log line via `caplog`). Forbidden assertions: `assert True`, `assert obj is obj`, `assert <constant> == <constant>`, `assert len(x) >= 0`, `assert isinstance(x, <statically-known-type>)`, `mock.assert_called*` as the only assertion. Run `uv run pytest tests/<modified_file>::<test_name> -v` after each strengthening to confirm the test passes with the new assertion. Do NOT run the full suite — that's S10/S11's job. Do NOT re-run the scanner here — S02 owns the baseline rewrite. Do NOT touch any production code outside `tests/**` — if a strengthening requires a production-code change, DELETE the test and raise a blocker. Do NOT touch the 7 `mock-only` entries — that's S02's scope. Do NOT touch the 548 `tautology` entries — out of scope for this CR. 2400 s timeout. Read `tests/CLAUDE.md` and `skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md` before starting.

Strengthen the 7 `mock-only` entries (dump via `grep '# mock-only$' tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt`). Default action is CONVERT — rewrite to assert on a real observable (return value, DB row, log line via `caplog`); the mock may remain to isolate the unit but is no longer the only assertion. DELETE permitted only with a one-line rationale naming the covering test. Same forbidden-assertion list as S01. Run `uv run pytest tests/<modified_file>::<test_name> -v` after each conversion. Then re-run the scanner to shrink the baseline: `uv run python scripts/check_test_assertions.py --write-baseline tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt tests/`. Verify: `grep -c '# no-assert$' tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt` returns 0; `grep -c '# mock-only$' tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt` returns 0; `grep -c '# tautology$' tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt` returns 548 (untouched). Verify `make test-assertions` exits 0. Then update `ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md`: §5 row `P1-CR-A-followup` to record '78 of 626 baseline entries strengthened in this CR (CR-00081, 2026-05-24); remaining 548 tautology entries deferred to future per-module CRs'; v1.3 header status block's baseline counts from `626 entries: 71 no-assert / 7 mock-only / 548 tautology` to `~548 entries: 0 no-assert / 0 mock-only / 548 tautology` (noting CR-00081); add a new §11 changelog entry dated 2026-05-24 with the 78-entry strengthening summary, the strengthen/delete/convert split, and a forward link from CR-00046's entry. Do NOT touch any production code outside `tests/**`, the baseline file, and the tracker.

Review S01: for each strengthened/converted test among the 71 no-assert entries, verify the new assertion is real and behaviour-pinning (apply the mutation-test question: would this test fail if the specific production line it covers regressed?). Reject any assertion of the forbidden form (`assert True`, `assert obj is obj`, `assert <constant> == <constant>`, `mock.assert_called*` as sole assertion, `assert len(x) >= 0`, `assert isinstance(x, <statically-known-type>)`). For every DELETE: verify a one-line rationale is present AND the named covering test actually covers the same surface (open it and check). Verify scope: only files under `tests/**` modified — NO production code edits, NO migrations, NO `tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt` edits (S02 owns that), NO tracker edits (S02 owns those). Verify each modified test passes via targeted `uv run pytest tests/<file>::<test> -v`. Pre-review gate: `make lint` + `make format-check` show zero new violations on the modified test files.

Review S02: for each of the 7 strengthened/converted mock-only tests, verify the new assertion is on a real observable (not `mock.assert_called*` again — that would be a no-op conversion). Same forbidden-assertion list as S03. Verify baseline rewrite: `grep -c '# no-assert$' tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt` = 0; `grep -c '# mock-only$' tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt` = 0; `grep -c '# tautology$' tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt` = 548 (untouched — if the tautology count moved, raise a CRITICAL finding because it means S02 touched out-of-scope tests). Verify `make test-assertions` passes. Verify tracker edits: §5 row records 78 of 626 with CR-00081 + 2026-05-24; v1.3 header counts updated to ~548 / 0 / 0 / 548; §11 has a new dated changelog entry with strengthen/delete/convert split and forward link from CR-00046. Verify scope: only `tests/**`, `tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt`, `ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md` modified.

Global cross-agent review covering AC1–AC6. AC1: baseline has 0 no-assert + 0 mock-only + 548 tautology after S02's rewrite. AC2: spot-check 10 random strengthened tests and confirm each carries a real, specific, behaviour-pinning assertion (run the mutation-test question in your head; for at least 2 of the 10, also stash-revert the production line you believe the test covers, re-run the test, confirm it goes red, restore — only if it's safe to do so in this worktree). AC3: every DELETE in S01/S02 reports has a one-line rationale with a named covering test. AC4: diff against main touches only `tests/**`, `tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt`, `ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md` — no `orch/`, `dashboard/`, `executor/`, `scripts/`, `bin/`, `templates/`, `skills/`, `.claude/skills/`, `.github/`. AC5: tracker §5 row + v1.3 header + §11 changelog all consistent — same counts (78), same date (2026-05-24), same CR-ID (CR-00081), and the strengthen/delete/convert split adds up to 78. AC6 (covered by S06–S13 gates). No scope creep (no rule changes in the testing skill, no scanner changes, no `pyproject.toml` edits). Run `make test-assertions` + `make test-unit` + targeted re-runs of any modified file. `make check` NOT run here — that's S10/S11/S12.
QV: Assertion scanner — the regression-net signal. Must pass because the baseline has been shrunk by exactly the entries addressed, so no NEW violations beyond the baseline appear.
QV: Unit tests (must pass under the strengthened assertions)
QV: Integration tests (must pass under the strengthened assertions)
QV: Secret scanning (gitleaks) — no secrets expected; tests-only change

Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill. Look for: (a) did S01 or S02 hit a strengthening that surfaced a real production-code bug (the design's notes section flagged this risk); (b) was the strengthen/delete/convert split close to design expectations (heavy STRENGTHEN, occasional DELETE, rare CONVERT for no-assert); (c) did any QV gate burn a fix cycle and if so why; (d) compare against CR-00046's baseline-establishment patterns and CR-00045's tdd_red_evidence patterns.
